statistics.name: assists
statistics.description: The number of times a player who passes the ball to a teammate in a way that leads to a score by field goal, meaning that he or she was "assisting" in the basket. There is some judgment involved in deciding whether a passer should be credited with an assist.

statistics.name: turnovers
statistics.description: The number of times a player loses possession to the other team.

statistics.description: The total number of minutes played.

statistics.description: The average number of assists a player or team records per turnover

statistics.name: rebounds
statistics.description: The total number of rebounds (offensive and defensive).

statistics.name: defensiveRebounds
statistics.description: The number of times when the defense obtains the possession of the ball after a missed shot by the offense.

statistics.name: offensiveRebounds
statistics.description: The number of times when the offense obtains the possession of the ball after a missed shot.

articles.headline: Chicago hosts Seattle following Hiedeman's 31-point game
articles.description: Seattle Storm (6-19, 1-12 Western Conference) at Chicago Sky (7-16, 1-7 Eastern Conference)

articles.headline: Golden State takes on Indiana, seeks 5th straight road win
articles.description: Golden State Valkyries (17-7, 7-5 Western Conference) at Indiana Fever (14-9, 5-4 Eastern Conference)

articles.headline: Los Angeles visits Minnesota following McBride's 37-point game
articles.description: Los Angeles Sparks (10-12, 5-6 Western Conference) at Minnesota Lynx (18-6, 11-1 Western Conference)

articles.headline: Lynx rookie Miles reaches 400 points, 100 rebounds, 100 assists
articles.description: Minnesota rookie Olivia Miles scored a career-high 33, becoming the first WNBA player to reach 400 points, 100 rebounds and 100 assists in only 22 career games, and the Lynx defeated the Phoenix Mercury 104-100 Monday night for a third straight victory.

images.name: Olivia Miles scores career-high 33 points in Lynx's win over the Mercury
images.caption: Olivia Miles scores career-high 33 points in Lynx's win over the Mercury

articles.headline: Kayla McBride, Olivia Miles star in the Lynx's 104-100 win over the Mercury
articles.description: — Kayla McBride scored a season-high 37 points, rookie Olivia Miles had a career-high 33 and the Minnesota Lynx beat the Phoenix Mercury 104-100 on Monday night for their third straight victory.

images.name: Sami Whitcomb knocks down the 3-pointer
images.caption: Sami Whitcomb knocks down the 3-pointer

articles.headline: Minnesota Lynx vs. Phoenix Mercury - Game Highlights
articles.description: Watch the Game Highlights from Minnesota Lynx vs. Phoenix Mercury, 07/13/2026

images.name: Minnesota Lynx vs. Phoenix Mercury - Game Highlights
images.caption: Watch the Game Highlights from Minnesota Lynx vs. Phoenix Mercury, 07/13/2026

articles.headline: Dream beat the Sparks 101-92 behind Angel Reese's WNBA-leading 16th double-double
articles.description: — Angel Reese had 23 points and 13 rebounds for her WNBA-leading 16th double-double of the season, Allisha Gray added 20 points, and the Atlanta Dream beat the Los Angeles Sparks 101-92 on Monday night.

images.name: Atlanta Dream vs. Los Angeles Sparks - Game Highlights
images.caption: Watch the Game Highlights from Atlanta Dream vs. Los Angeles Sparks, 07/13/2026

articles.headline: Atlanta Dream vs. Los Angeles Sparks - Game Highlights
articles.description: Watch the Game Highlights from Atlanta Dream vs. Los Angeles Sparks, 07/13/2026

images.name: Atlanta Dream vs. Los Angeles Sparks - Game Highlights
images.caption: Watch the Game Highlights from Atlanta Dream vs. Los Angeles Sparks, 07/13/2026

articles.headline: 2026 WNBA championship odds: Valkyries now fourth-shortest on futures market
articles.description: Entering the week at 17-7, the Golden State Valkyries now have the fourth-shortest odds to win the WNBA title.

images.name: Gabby Williams  [1296x729]
images.caption: Gabby Williams leads the Valkyries in scoring with 15.0 PPG.
images.alt: Gabby Williams #1 of the Golden State Valkyries dribbles the ball during the game =a  on July 6, 2026 at  CareFirst Arena in Washington, D.C.

images.name: Olivia Miles [1296x729]
images.caption: Olivia Miles and the Minnesota Lynx are now alone in front as favorites to win the WNBA championship.
images.alt: Minnesota Lynx guard Olivia Miles (5) dribbles past Dallas Wings guard Azzi Fudd (35) during the WNBA game between the Dallas Wings and the Minnesota Lynx on June 28, 2026, at College Park Center in Arlington, TX.

images.name: Natasha Howard of Minnesota Lynx [608x342]
images.caption: A strong start to the season has the Lynx tied with the Aces for the shortest title odds, just ahead of the Liberty.

images.name: Olivia Miles [1296x729]
images.caption: The Lynx have seen their odds shorten but have a tough schedule this week.
images.alt: Minnesota Lynx guard Olivia Miles (5) drives past Dallas Wings guard Paige Bueckers during the second half of a WNBA basketball game in Arlington, Texas, Thursday, May 14, 2026.

images.name: Olivia Miles [1296x729]
images.caption: Rookie of the Year favorite Olivia Miles is helping drive the early success of the Minnesota Lynx.

images.name: Paige Bueckers [1296x729]
images.caption: Paige Bueckers and her 19.3 PPG is a big reason for the encouraging start by the Wings.
images.alt: Dallas Wings guard Paige Bueckers (5) drives to the basket against Chicago Sky guard Natasha Cloud (9) during the second half at Wintrust Arena.

images.name: A'ja Wilson [1296x729]
images.caption: The Las Vegas Aces, led by reigning MVP A'ja Wilson, are again one of the favorites to win the WNBA championship this season.
images.alt: Las Vegas Aces center A'ja Wilson (22) celebrates a play with teammates during the first half of a WNBA preseason basketball game against the Dallas Wings in Austin, Texas, Sunday, May 3, 2026.

images.name: Satou Sabally [1296x729]
images.caption: The New York Liberty made some free agent moves, most notably signing Satou Sabally.

images.name: Angel Reese [1296x729]
images.caption: Angel Reese heads to Atlanta after two seasons in Chicago.
images.alt: Chicago Sky forward Angel Reese (5) dribbles against the Atlanta Dream in the first quarter at Gateway Center Arena at College Park.

articles.headline: Fantasy women's hoops: Gustafson, Lacan and more players to add this week
articles.description: With one full week left before the All-Star break, here are four players to help you win your weekly matchup.

images.name: Megan Gustafson [1296x729]
images.caption: Megan Gustafson of the Portland Fire is shooting .592 from the field in July.

images.name: Leila Lacan [608x342]
images.caption: The Sun are struggling, but Leila Lacan continues to put up useful numbers for fantasy.

images.name: A'ja Wilson [1296x729]
images.caption: A'ja Wilson is the leading scorer in fantasy women's basketball this season.

images.name: Jessica Shepard gets her 3rd triple-double of the season
images.caption: Jessica Shepard gets her 3rd triple-double of the season

articles.headline: Connecticut faces Portland on 3-game home skid
articles.description: Portland Fire (10-13, 3-6 Western Conference) at Connecticut Sun (5-18, 2-8 Eastern Conference)

articles.headline: Washington visits Toronto after Mabrey's 30-point game
articles.description: Washington Mystics (11-10, 7-5 Eastern Conference) at Toronto Tempo (10-13, 5-6 Eastern Conference)

articles.headline: Fever beat Aces again as Caitlin Clark scores 12 in 24 minutes
articles.description: The Fever beat the defending champion Aces in Las Vegas for the second time in eight days. Caitlin Clark, who has been dealing with back issues, played 24 minutes and finished with 12 points, seven rebounds and six assists.

images.name: Las Vegas Aces vs. Indiana Fever - Game Highlights
images.caption: Watch the Game Highlights from Las Vegas Aces vs. Indiana Fever, 07/12/2026

articles.headline: Kelsey Mitchell, Caitlin Clark lead Fever to 109-75 win over A'ja Wilson and the Aces
articles.description: — Caitlin Clark had 12 points, seven rebounds and six assists, Kelsey Mitchell scored 27 points, and the Indiana Fever beat A'ja Wilson and the Las Vegas Aces 109-75 on Sunday night.

images.name: Las Vegas Aces vs. Indiana Fever - Game Highlights

article.headline: Hiedeman scores 24, Johnson adds 17 and Fam 10 in her debut as Storm beat Mystics 97-85
article.description: — Natisha Hiedeman tied her career high with 24 points, rookie Flau’jae Johnson scored a season-best 17, and the Seattle Storm beat the Washington Mystics 97-85 on Sunday in a game that featured 75 total free throws, including 52 in the second half.

article.story: SEATTLE -- — Natisha Hiedeman tied her career high with 24 points, rookie Flau’jae Johnson scored a season-best 17, and the Seattle Storm beat the Washington Mystics 97-85 on Sunday in a game that featured 75 total free throws, including 52 in the second half. Seattle's Awa Fam, the No. 3 overall draft pick, made her WNBA debut and finished with 10 points in 20 minutes. The 6-foot-4 center, who turns 20 on June 17, joined the Storm on Friday after helping Valencia win the Spanish League title. She scored her first WNBA points with a layup off a pick-and-roll with Johnson late in the second quarter. Seattle made a season-high 13 3-pointers on 28 attempts and hit 32 of 34 (94%) shots from the line. The Mystics hit 35 of 41 (85%) from the foul line, and shot just 20% (4 of 20) from beyond the arc. Stefanie Dolson scored 16 points with perfect shooting. She went 3 of 3 from the field, 2 of 2 from 3-point range, and 8 of 8 from the line for the Storm (3-4). Zia Cooke added 10 points. Dominique Malonga, who leads the Storm in scoring (16.0 points per game) and rebounds (7.3), missed her fourth consecutive game due to the concussion protocol. Sonia Citron led the Mystics (2-3) with 16 points. Kiki Iriafen, Georgia Amoore and Angela Dugalic added 13 points apiece, and Shakira Austin had 12. Hiedeman hit a 3-pointer that gave Seattle the lead for good early in the second quarter, and her long floater with 1.8 seconds left in the period made it 49-37. The Mystics trailed by double figures throughout the second half. Johnson made 7 of 12 from the field and finished with seven rebounds and five assists. Seattle beat Connecticut 77-59 on Friday to snap a three-game skid. Up next The teams play again Wednesday in Seattle. ------ AP WNBA: https://apnews.com/hub/wnba-basketball.
